query: I've got a gnarly migration: ~40 services need their logging swapped over to structlog, and we need to redesign the log schema first. Act as tech lead — plan it, split the design work vs the grunt work across your subagents, and get a second opinion from codex on the schema before anything moves.

this refactor is huge (auth + billing + notifications, like 8k lines total). don't burn your own context reading everything — delegate the recon and farm the mechanical edits out to cheaper models. show me the plan first
I want a cross-vendor sanity check on our proposed sharding key for the events table: have opus and codex look at it independently, blind to each other, then reconcile their takes for me
orchestrate this one: fix the flaky retry test in ci, then extract the retry/backoff logic into its own lib with tests. use your worker agents where it makes sense, keep the expensive model usage low
